channels.name: Fox
channels.note: 70 matches including all USMNT games, semis & final. Free OTA with antenna.

channels.name: FS1
channels.note: 34 matches. Requires cable or streaming TV subscription.

channels.name: Telemundo
channels.note: 92 matches in Spanish (12 more on UNIVERSO). Free OTA with antenna.

channels.name: Peacock
channels.note: Streams Telemundo's Spanish-language coverage of all 104 matches. Subscription required ($7.99/mo).

channels.name: Tubi
channels.note: Opening ceremonies + 2 matches in 4K free: Mexico v South Africa (Jun 11) and USMNT v Paraguay (Jun 12).

channels.name: FOX One
channels.note: All 104 matches via FOX One streaming. New Fox direct-to-consumer service.

channels.name: FOX Sports App
channels.note: All 104 matches with cable login. 4K available.

channels.name: UNIVERSO
channels.note: 12 matches in Spanish. Requires cable.

channels.name: Canal 5 / Las Estrellas
channels.note: 32 matches free-to-air via TelevisaUnivision. Includes all Mexico matches, final, and select knockout games.

channels.name: TV Azteca
channels.note: 32 matches free on Azteca Uno / Azteca 7. Same 32 key matches as Canal 5. All Mexico games + final included.

channels.name: TUDN
channels.note: Full tournament coverage via cable (TelevisaUnivision). Studio analysis and pre/post-match shows.

channels.name: ViX+
channels.note: All 104 matches via ViX+ premium streaming. 72 matches exclusive to ViX+ (not on free TV). Subscription required.

channels.name: CTV
channels.note: Free-to-air English coverage from Bell Media. All 104 matches air across CTV/TSN/RDS combined.

channels.name: TSN
channels.note: English. Full Bell Media coverage of all 104 matches. Stream via TSN+, TSN GO, CTV GO.

channels.name: RDS
channels.note: French Bell Media coverage. Stream via RDS Go.

channels.name: Globo
channels.note: 54 matches across TV Globo (free), SporTV (cable) and Globoplay (streaming). All Brazil matches + Final.

channels.name: CazéTV
channels.note: All 104 matches free on YouTube (50% exclusive). Hosted by Casimiro Miguel.

channels.name: SBT
channels.note: 32 matches free, simulcast on NSports streaming. Return after ~30 years away from the World Cup.

channels.name: Telefe
channels.note: 32 matches free-to-air, including all Argentina games.

channels.name: TV Pública
channels.note: 10 matches free, including all Argentina games and the Final.

channels.name: DSPORTS
channels.note: All 104 matches via DirecTV Sports. 71-73 matches exclusive to DSPORTS.

channels.name: Win Sports
channels.note: Pay TV coverage.

channels.name: Chilevisión
channels.note: Exclusive Chilean rights — all matches free.

channels.name: Canal 5
channels.note: Free-to-air national broadcaster.

channels.name: Antel TV
channels.note: Pay TV via state telecom Antel.

channels.name: América Televisión
channels.note: Primary Peruvian broadcaster for the tournament. Verified 2026-04-17.

channels.name: DirecTV Latin America
channels.note: Regional pay-TV coverage across Latin America.

channels.name: Teletica
channels.note: Free-to-air coverage.

channels.name: Tigo Sports
channels.note: Pay TV coverage.

channels.name: Medcom
channels.note: Includes RPC and COS channels.

channels.name: Tigo Sports
channels.note: All 104 matches live. Verified 2026-04-17.

channels.name: TVJ
channels.note: Television Jamaica holds exclusive free-to-air rights for Jamaica (announced April 2026). Live matches on TVJ + on-air analysis. Verified 2026-05-05.

channels.name: BBC
channels.note: Shared 50/50 with ITV (104 matches total). Free on BBC One/iPlayer. Final simulcast Jul 19.

channels.name: ITV
channels.note: Shared 50/50 with BBC. Includes England v Croatia opener (Jun 17). Free on ITV1/ITV4/ITVX. Final simulcast.

channels.name: ARD
channels.note: 30 matches free-to-air

channels.name: ZDF
channels.note: 30 matches free-to-air

channels.name: MagentaTV
channels.note: All 104 matches (44 exclusive). Telekom.

channels.name: M6
channels.note: 54 free matches including all France games. TF1 holds zero matches for the first time since 1978.

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: All 104 matches with subscription.

channels.name: RTVE
channels.note: All Spain matches + key tournament games on La 1 (free).

channels.name: DAZN
channels.note: Exclusive pay-TV rights to all 104 matches in Spain (via Mediapro deal).

channels.name: Movistar Plus+
channels.note: 71 matches available via DAZN partnership for Movistar subscribers.

channels.name: RAI
channels.note: 35 matches free on Rai 1, including the opening match, semi-finals and final.

channels.name: DAZN
channels.note: All 104 matches via streaming. 69 exclusive to DAZN.

channels.name: NOS
channels.note: All matches free on NPO 1 / NPO 3 and NOS.nl live stream. NOS is sole Dutch rights holder — no paid alternative needed.

channels.name: Sport TV
channels.note: All 104 matches with subscription. 70 matches exclusive to Sport TV.

channels.name: LiveModeTV / CazéTV
channels.note: 34 matches free on YouTube including all Portugal national team games. Same operator as Brazil's CazéTV.

channels.name: VRT
channels.note: Primary FIFA licensee for Belgium. Free Dutch coverage on Eén, Canvas and Sporza streaming. Verified 2026-05-05.

channels.name: RTBF
channels.note: All 104 matches free in French on La Une, Tipik, La Trois and Auvio streaming. Verified 2026-05-05.

channels.name: TRT
channels.note: Exclusive rights for 2026 FIFA World Cup. Full free-to-air coverage.

channels.name: RTS
channels.note: Free-to-air coverage.

channels.name: Arena Sport
channels.note: Pay TV coverage.

channels.name: SRF
channels.note: German-language coverage on SRF zwei and SRF Play. Part of SRG SSR. All matches free.

channels.name: RTS
channels.note: French-language coverage on RTS 2 and RTS Play. Part of SRG SSR.

channels.name: RSI
channels.note: Italian-language coverage on RSI LA 2. Part of SRG SSR. All three language regions get full free coverage.

channels.name: ORF
channels.note: 52 matches free on ORF 1 and ORF ON streaming. Includes opening match, final, and Austria's 1st + 3rd group matches (if qualified).

channels.name: ServusTV
channels.note: 52 matches free on ServusTV and servustv.com. Sublicensed from ORF. Austria's 2nd group match (if qualified). All 104 matches free across ORF + ServusTV.

channels.name: NRK
channels.note: ~52 matches free on NRK1 and NRK TV (streaming). Includes Norway vs Senegal, Norway vs France, and the World Cup Final.

channels.name: TV 2
channels.note: ~52 matches. Norway's opener vs Iraq on TV 2. All matches free on TV 2 or TV 2 Play (some require Play subscription).

channels.name: SVT
channels.note: Free on SVT1/SVT2 and SVT Play. Shares rights 50/50 with TV4. Key Sweden matches on SVT including Sweden vs Tunisia.

channels.name: TV4
channels.note: Free on TV4 and TV4 Play. Owns a larger share of total matches. All 104 matches available across SVT + TV4 combined.

channels.name: DR
channels.note: ~52 matches free on DR1 and DRTV. Shares rights equally with TV 2. All Denmark matches split between DR and TV 2.

channels.name: TV 2
channels.note: ~52 matches on TV 2 and TV 2 Play. All 104 matches available free across DR + TV 2 combined.

channels.name: Yle
channels.note: 40 matches free on Yle TV2 and Yle Areena. Shares rights equally with MTV3. Finland national team matches alternate between Yle and MTV3.

channels.name: MTV3
channels.note: 40 matches free on MTV3 and MTV Katsomo. All 80 main matches available free across Yle + MTV3 combined.

channels.name: RÚV
channels.note: All matches free on RÚV (Sjónvarpið) and RÚV 2. Also streaming free on ruv.is and the RÚV app. Rights secured via EBU deal covering 2026 + 2030 World Cups. Radio coverage on Rás 1 and Rás 2.

channels.name: RTÉ
channels.note: Free on RTÉ2 and RTÉ Player. Long-term FIFA rights holder in Ireland. All key matches including opening match, knockouts, and final.

channels.name: Antena
channels.note: Romanian rights holder for the tournament.

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: Canal+
channels.note: Francophone Africa

channels.name: Canal+
channels.note: Francophone Africa

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: Canal+
channels.note: Francophone Africa

channels.name: SABC
channels.note: Selected matches

channels.name: NHK
channels.note: 33 matches free-to-air.

channels.name: Nippon TV
channels.note: 15 matches free-to-air.

channels.name: Fuji TV
channels.note: 10 matches free-to-air.

channels.name: DAZN
channels.note: All 104 matches via streaming. Subscription required.

channels.name: JTBC
channels.note: Exclusive Korean rights ($125M paid). Resale talks with KBS/MBC/SBS Korea Pool deadlocked — all matches may require paid access.

channels.name: Naver Sports / CHZZK
channels.note: Digital streaming via JTBC partnership.

channels.name: SSC
channels.note: Saudi Sports Company

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: beIN headquarters

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: Official FIFA licensee for the MENA region (satellite). Verified 2026-04-17.

channels.name: IRIB
channels.note: Iranian state broadcaster — traditional free-to-air coverage of World Cup matches. Check IRIB Varzesh (TV3) schedule closer to kick-off.

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: TBD
channels.note: Rights still unsold as of April 2026. FIFA cut asking price from $100M to $35M. JioStar reportedly negotiating around $25M. FIFA+ digital-first distribution is a possible fallback if no broadcaster signs.

channels.name: TVRI
channels.note: Exclusive Indonesian rights. All 104 matches free-to-air on TVRI Sport and TVRI Nasional. Major change from 2022 (Emtek Group / Vidio).

channels.name: Thai PBS
channels.note: Selected matches

channels.name: beIN Sports
channels.note: MENA rights

channels.name: Zo'r TV
channels.note: Uzbek national broadcaster — free-to-air coverage.

channels.name: SBS
channels.note: Exclusive Australian rights. All 104 matches free across SBS, SBS VICELAND, and SBS On Demand.

channels.name: TVNZ
channels.note: All Whites matches and select games free-to-air on TVNZ. (Sky Sport NZ lost rights — held them in 2022.)

channels.name: TVNZ+
channels.note: Full tournament access via event pass on TVNZ+ streaming. TVNZ's first paid offering.
